subject: Geology
title: Luster and Color
description: A micro-lesson that makes Luster and Color usable.

hook.text: Pyrite looks like gold. It fooled thousands of miners. Color alone is the most unreliable way to name a mineral.

buildup.text: Luster describes how light reflects from a mineral: metallic, glassy, waxy, pearly, silky, or dull.

discovery.text: Quartz can be clear, purple, pink, or smoky. Tiny impurities change color, but luster stays glassy.

twist.text: Luster is far more reliable than color for identification. A mineral's surface shine rarely changes with impurities.

climax.text: Experienced geologists check luster before color. The way light plays on a surface reveals the mineral's nature.

punchline.text: Don't trust the color; trust the shine.

quiz.question: Why is color unreliable for mineral identification?
options: Tiny impurities can change a mineral's color
All minerals are the same color
